claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child has started an apprenticeship in England
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To assess this, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children you'd like to claim Child Benefit for? If so, could you tell me their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child – my son Alex. He's 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Since Alex is 17, I need to understand his current situation to determine your eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as school, college, university, or an apprenticeship)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's just started an apprenticeship recently. He's doing a training course.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I need to ask a bit more to ensure I have the full picture.

**Is Alex's apprenticeship in England?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. He's doing his apprenticeship here in England.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I appreciate you providing that detail. I need to check one more important thing about Alex's employment situation.

**Is Alex working 24 hours or more per week as part of his apprenticeship?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working that many hours. It's less than that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I have a clearer picture. Let me ask one final question to complete my assessment:

**Are you living in the UK with Alex, or are you making financial contributions towards his upkeep (such as food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and Alex lives here at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary

Unfortunately, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

The reason is that Alex has started an apprenticeship in England. The Child Benefit rules specifically state that you'll stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. This applies even though Alex is under 20 and living with you in the UK.

If you have any other children you'd like me to assess, or if you have further questions about this decision, please let me know.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and has recently started an apprenticeship in England. According to the Child Benefit rules, you stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. Since Alex has just begun his apprenticeship, you are no longer eligible to claim Child Benefit for him, regardless of the number of hours he works or that he lives with you.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because he has started an apprenticeship in England. The rules state that Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, which is a specific disqualifying circumstance even for children under 20 who would otherwise qualify through approved education or training.">

Since Alex has just begun his apprenticeship, you are no longer eligible to claim Child Benefit for him, regardless of the number of hours he works or that he lives with you.

The rules state that Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, which is a specific disqualifying circumstance even for children under 20 who would otherwise qualify through approved education or training.">
